content: Father said I'd lead the procession and lead the procession I did--to the displeasure of my two sisters, both smiles ruled with envy. Well, one more than the other. I had the center bedroom in our house, the center drawer in the bathroom, the center cubby hole for my shoes, and I centered the procession on this most pleasant feast day. I naturally fell in the center of all things, it wasn't a choice, but more of a natural selection of proceedings. I didn't ask for this, and who would? To be the disdain of my siblings, their hateful eyes alway glaring and gleaming with the peak interest in seeing me collapse and crumble underneath the weight of my beauty--or rather the shadow of their collective beauty. This is the game we've played, we are kind and gentle to one another and have been since memory had found us. Kind and gentle to the ledge, and then so kindly and so gently to be shoved into crashing bone crushing death. I suppose I'm most gullible, not really realizing the depth of their jealousy until repeated offenses and improper orders of operations struck me enough to settle the seed of eye opening knowledge into the depths of my view. They hate me, my face, the way I
